title: Unit 1: Lecture 1–7
text: In this section of Unit 1, you will learn what communication and language are. You’ll learn the theories of language evolution, appreciate the differences between human and animal communication, and the relationship between society and language.

title: B1.4 –– Introduction to Linguistics
text:
This course will give you a brief tour of linguistics.
You will learn how language is a window to the mind. We will discuss evolution of language, animal and human communication, and learn about sounds of language, grammar, how meanings are formed and represented in the mind among many other things.
As speech pathology and audiology undergrads, you will learn to connect these ideas from linguistics to the disorders of speech, language, and hearing.
You will read textbooks as well as scientific papers from academic journals assigned before and after each lecture.

Select Academic Year/Term:
 2083

title: Extra Readings
text: Here you will find papers relevant to the topics we discussed in the class.

title: Extra Readings
text:
May 25, 2026
At what age do children develop theory of mind?

Paper 1Paper 2


This is a seminal paper which set the experimental paradigm on examining theory of mind of young children.
Wimmer, H., & Perner, J. (1983). Beliefs about beliefs: Representation and constraining function of wrong beliefs in young children’s understanding of deception. Cognition, 13(1), 103-128.






Developed on the first paper, this paper examines the theory of mind of children with autism.
Baron-Cohen, S., Leslie, A. M., & Frith, U. (1985). Does the autistic child have a “theory of mind”?. Cognition, 21(1), 37-46.
